alerts.title: Added: GCC Labelling Requirement - General labelling requirements
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Verification required. General labelling requirements for prepackaged food, including but not limited to ingredient lists, nutritional information, net content, manufacturer details, and potentially allergen declarations, must be verified against the full text of this technical regulation.

alerts.title: Added: GCC Labelling Requirement - General requirements for Halal food
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Verification required. General requirements for food to be considered Halal must be verified against the full text of this technical regulation. This impacts ingredients, processing, and potentially labelling if a Halal claim is made.

alerts.title: Added: GCC Labelling Requirement - Mandatory expiration dates
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Verification required. Mandatory expiration date requirements for food products must be verified against the full text of this technical regulation.

alerts.title: Added: GCC Microbiology Limits - Microbiological limits
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Verification required. Specific microbiological limits for various foodstuffs and food ingredients must be verified against the full text of this technical regulation.

alerts.title: Added: Singapore Labelling Requirement - Source fetch / extraction issue
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Manual review required. Automated extraction failed: {"error":{"code":503,"message":"This model is currently experiencing high demand. Spikes in demand are usually temporary. Please try again later.","status":"UNAVAILABLE"}}

alerts.title: Added: Taiwan Additive Limits - γ-Aminobutyric Acid (GABA)
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Verification required. Specific use restrictions and labeling requirements for γ-Aminobutyric Acid (GABA) as a food ingredient must be verified.

alerts.title: Added: Taiwan Allergen List - Crustacean shellfish, mango, peanuts, sesame seeds, milk and goat milk, eggs, tree nuts, gluten-containing cereals, soybeans, fish, and sulphite
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: 10. Sulphite labeling is mandatory if the residue sulphur dioxide is 10 or more milligrams per kilogram of food.

alerts.title: Added: Taiwan Allergen List - Mandatory Allergens
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Check official source. Food allergen labeling is required for prepackaged foods that contain any of the specified 11 allergens as an ingredient or food additive.

alerts.title: Added: Taiwan Contaminant Limits - Pesticide Residues
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Verification required. Specific limits for various pesticides in different food categories must be verified against the full standard.

alerts.title: Added: Taiwan Labelling Requirement - Nutrition Claims
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Check official source. Prepackaged food products must comply with revised regulations on nutrition claims.

alerts.title: Added: Thailand Labelling Requirement - Prohibited foods
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Prohibited. Certain foods are prohibited from production, import, or sale. Verification of prohibited food lists is required.

alerts.title: Added: United States Additive Limits - Prohibited substances
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Prohibited. Prohibited substances are listed in FDA regulations (21 CFR Part 189) and labeled as 'PROHIBITED' or 'PROHIBITED WITH EXCEPTIONS'.

alerts.title: Added: United States Allergen List - Major Food Allergens
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Milk, Eggs, Fish, Crustacean shellfish, Tree nuts, Peanuts, Wheat, Soybeans, Sesame. The FASTER Act (effective January 1, 2023) declared sesame as the 9th major food allergen. These allergens account for 90 percent of food allergies and serious allergic reactions in the U.S.

alerts.title: Added: United States Contaminant Limits - 3-Monochloropropane-1,2-Diol (3-MCPD)
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: 1. Guidance Level for 3-MCPD.

alerts.title: Added: United States Contaminant Limits - Aflatoxins, total
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: 20. Action Level for total Aflatoxins.

alerts.title: Added: United States Contaminant Limits - Chemical Contaminants (Tolerances, Action Levels, Guidance Levels)
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Verification required. Manufacturers must check the Chemical Contaminants Transparency Tool for specific tolerances, action levels, guidance levels, derived intervention levels, recommended maximum levels, and advisory levels for poisonous or deleterious substances in human food. Tolerances and action levels are defined in 21 CFR parts 109 and 509.

alerts.title: Added: United States Labelling Requirement - Allergen Preventive Controls
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Compliance with 21 CFR part 117. The 'Current Good Manufacturing Practice, Hazard Analysis, and Risk-Based Preventive Controls for Human Food' rule (CGMP & PC rule, 21 CFR part 117) establishes requirements for allergen preventive controls to prevent allergen cross-contact in manufacturing and packaging and to prevent undeclared allergens.

alerts.title: Added: United States Labelling Requirement - Cochineal extract and carmine
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Mandatory declaration. Color additives made from cochineal extract and carmine must be declared on the label.

alerts.title: Added: United States Labelling Requirement - FD&C Yellow No. 5
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Mandatory declaration. The FDA requires all products containing FD&C Yellow No. 5 to identify it on their labels.

alerts.title: Added: United States Labelling Requirement - Gluten-free claim
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Defined standard for gluten content. The FDA issued a final rule defining 'gluten-free' for food labeling. A separate rule (August 12, 2020) establishes compliance requirements for fermented and hydrolyzed foods bearing the 'gluten-free' claim.

alerts.title: Added: United States Labelling Requirement - Major Food Allergen Declaration
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Mandatory. FALCPA requires foods or ingredients containing a 'major food allergen' to be specifically labeled with the name of the allergen source. This applies to most packaged food products, including dietary supplements.

alerts.title: Added: United States Labelling Requirement - Major Food Allergen Declaration Format
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Mandatory. The allergen's food source must be declared at least once on the food label either in parentheses following the ingredient name (e.g., 'lecithin (soy)') or immediately after or next to the ingredient list in a 'contains' statement (e.g., 'Contains wheat, milk, and soy.').

alerts.title: Added: United States Labelling Requirement - Specific Allergen Declaration
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Mandatory. The type of tree nut (e.g., almonds, pecans, walnuts), and the species of fish (e.g., bass, flounder, cod) and Crustacean shellfish (e.g., crab, lobster, shrimp) must be declared.

alerts.title: Added: United States Labelling Requirement - Sulfiting agents (e.g., sodium bisulfite)
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: ≥10. Sulfiting agents must be declared on food labels when present in food at a concentration of ≥10 parts per million total sulfites.

alerts.title: Modified: China Additive Limits - Food Additives
alerts.details: Old: Verification required as per GB 2760-2015. New: Verification required against GB 2760-2015. The use of food additives must comply with the specified national standard. Specific limits and conditions are detailed within the standard.

alerts.title: Modified: Codex Additive Limits - Permitted food additives
alerts.details: Old: Verification required via GSFA Online Database. New: Verification required via GSFA database. The Codex General Standard for Food Additives (GSFA, Codex STAN 192-1995) sets forth the conditions under which permitted food additives may be used in all foods. Users are encouraged to consult the online database and its Preamble for specific provisions.

alerts.title: Added: Codex Contaminant Limits - Contaminants and naturally occurring toxicants
alerts.details: New: Verification required via Codex General Standard for Contaminants and Toxins in Food and Feed (CXS 193-1995) and CCCF established levels. Codex establishes Maximum Levels (MLs) for contaminants in food or feed commodities to protect human health, keeping levels as low as possible based on sound scientific evidence. The Codex Committee on Contaminants in Food (CCCF) establishes and endorses these permitted maximum levels or guideline levels.

alerts.title: Removed: Codex Contaminant Limits - Contaminants (e.g., mycotoxins, metals like arsenic, lead, mercury)
alerts.details: Old: Verification required via Codex General Standard for Contaminants and Toxins in Food and Feed (CXS 193-1995). Rule no longer appeared in the latest extraction. Verify whether source changed or extraction missed it.

alerts.title: Removed: Codex Contaminant Limits - Mycotoxins
alerts.details: Old: Follow Code of Practice for the Prevention and Reduction of Mycotoxins in Spices (CXC 78-2017). Rule no longer appeared in the latest extraction. Verify whether source changed or extraction missed it.

alerts.title: Added: European Union Additive Limits - Approved food additives and their conditions of use
alerts.details: New: Check official source. Verification required against the Union list of food additives in Annex II of Regulation (EC) No 1333/2008.

alerts.title: Removed: European Union Additive Limits - Food additives
alerts.details: Old: Verification required. Rule no longer appeared in the latest extraction. Verify whether source changed or extraction missed it.

alerts.title: Added: European Union Microbiology Limits - Competence of laboratories conducting shelf-life studies (challenge tests and durability studies) for Listeria monocytogenes
alerts.details: New: Check official source. Laboratories must comply with food safety criteria in Regulation (EC) No 2073/2005 and follow the EURL Lm Guidance Document for competence evaluation.

alerts.title: Added: European Union Microbiology Limits - Food safety criteria for relevant foodborne bacteria, toxins, metabolites (e.g., Salmonella, Listeria monocytogenes, Enterobacter sakazakii, staphylococcal enterotoxins, histamine) and process hygiene criteria
alerts.details: New: Check official source. Compliance required with Commission Regulation (EC) No 2073/2005.

alerts.title: Removed: European Union Microbiology Limits - Foodborne bacteria, toxins, and metabolites (e.g., Salmonella, Listeria monocytogenes, Enterobacter sakazakii, staphylococcal enterotoxins, histamine)
alerts.details: Old: Verification required. Rule no longer appeared in the latest extraction. Verify whether source changed or extraction missed it.

alerts.title: Added: GCC/GSO Labelling Requirement - General requirements for Halal food
alerts.details: New: Verification required as per GSO 2055-1:2015. This standard specifies the general requirements for Halal food, which may include labelling for Halal certification.

alerts.title: Added: GCC/GSO Labelling Requirement - Labelling of pre-packaged foodstuffs
alerts.details: New: Verification required as per GSO 9:2022. This standard specifies the general requirements for the labelling of pre-packaged foodstuffs.

alerts.title: Added: GCC/GSO Labelling Requirement - Mandatory shelf life for food products
alerts.details: New: Verification required as per GSO 150-1:2013. This standard specifies the mandatory shelf life periods for various food products.

alerts.title: Added: GCC/GSO Labelling Requirement - Quantity of product in pre-packaged packages
alerts.details: New: Verification required as per GSO OIML R87:2021. This standard specifies requirements for the quantity of product in pre-packaged packages, ensuring accurate net content declaration.

alerts.title: Added: GCC/GSO Labelling Requirement - Requirements for labels of packaged products
alerts.details: New: Verification required as per GSO OIML R79:2021. This standard specifies the requirements for labels of packaged products, ensuring accurate information for consumers.

alerts.title: Removed: GCC Labelling Requirement - General labelling requirements
alerts.details: Old: Verification required. Rule no longer appeared in the latest extraction. Verify whether source changed or extraction missed it.

alerts.details: New: Verification required as per FSSAI Compendium of Labelling & Display Regulations.. All general labelling and display requirements, including allergen declarations, nutritional information, and other warnings, must be verified against the detailed regulations.

alerts.title: Removed: India Labelling Requirement - General Labelling Requirements

alerts.details: New: Verification required for permitted additives and usage limits. Regulations exist for the use of food additives.

alerts.details: New: Verification required for specifications and standards for food additives. This document provides detailed specifications and standards for food additives.

alerts.title: Added: Japan Labelling Requirement - General Food Safety and Sanitation
alerts.details: New: Compliance with the Food Sanitation Law is required. This is a fundamental law governing food safety and sanitation in Japan.

alerts.title: Added: Japan Labelling Requirement - General Food Safety Principles
alerts.details: New: Compliance with the Food Safety Basic Law is required. This is a fundamental law establishing basic principles for food safety.

alerts.title: Added: Japan Labelling Requirement - Health Claims, Special Dietary Uses, Nutrition Labeling
alerts.details: New: Verification required for regulations on health claims, special dietary uses, and nutrition labeling. Specific regulations govern these types of food and their labeling.
